rationale: The cited paper appears only once, in the General Discussion, as a supporting example that social comparison can influence evaluations via perceived accomplishment. It is not used to develop hypotheses, structure the study designs, define key measures, or anchor the paper's main contribution. The core theoretical and empirical positioning is tied to queueing and goal-progress references, especially Zhou and Soman (2003). So this citation functions as a brief contextual mention rather than a substantive dependency.
